channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 194 replies — 361.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 194 • Updated: 2026-07-19 13:33 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496397
>>62496378
going up

62496401
>>62496397
in your dreams, stinkie
in your pathetic, loser-filled dreams

62496520
>>62492759
Yeah that's a fucken demo. Not a production use case.

62496533
>>62492372
>deeeemooooooo
whats that i hear?
>deeeeemoooooooo
it's the demo bell~
>deeeeeemoooooooooo
it means 5 more years of waiting until maybe real production use :}

62496544
>>62496373
>Hey guys, hear me out. What if we pretended that chopping up "assets" into fractional pieces was some sort of world changing idea? Like, you could chop up a loan into 50,000 pieces. How cool would THAT be?? It serves a very very real and practical purpose in the financial world. You will see.
Any actually useful financial vehicle is proposed and adopted almost immediately in the real world. Tokenization is just air sold to retards to keep the grift going just a little longer.

62496564
>deeeeeemoooooooooooo

62496570
>>62496401
Ok Nico.

62496684
>>62496520
Do you really think getting into a semantic debate with DTCC's wording is going to make you feel better about XRP not being involved at all?

62496690
Okay they've been live for a week, where's my $1000 LINK? Or even just my $100 LINK? Or perhaps even just my $10 LINK?

62496743
>>62496684
I don't invest in any crypto whatsoever. Every inch of it is for retards.

62496998
>>62496743
>Every inch of it is for retards.
Yeah, retards like DTCC, Swift, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, Nasdaq, NYSE, S&P Dow Jones Indices, the US Department of Commerce, and many many more.

62497078
>>62496544
You’re arguing against fractionalization, not tokenization. Fractional ownership has existed for decades through REITs, ETFs, ABS, and syndicated loans. Tokenization isn’t claiming to invent that. It’s an attempt to modernize the ownership and settlement rails. Whether it succeeds is an empirical question, not one answered by pointing out that fractionalization already exists. Many projects are hype, but that doesn’t make the underlying infrastructure worthless.

62497218
>>62496998
>Yeah, retards like DTCC, Swift, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, Nasdaq, NYSE, S&P Dow Jones Indices, the US Department of Commerce, and many many more.
Sure sure. They've all "expressed interest" or "have a team", but none of them are actually using it. It's just lip service to keep their fingers in many pies. I'll say it again, this is 100% useless, is going nowhere and is being used ONLY as star trek gibberish to keep greater fools on the hook. Using a public ledger (which is all crypto brings to the table) to do any of these things is utterly pointless when it can all be done via traditional digital systems at about 100000x the speed.

62497425
>>62497218
Wall of cope

62497444
>>62497425
p+c

62497473
>>62497218
/thread
link retards always resort to muh appeal to authority

62497528
>>62497218
>>62497473
settle down verbo, surely XRP will be involved next time (lol)

62497544
>>62497528
p+c

62497604
>>62497528
>61 pbtid
>"settle down"
lel the irony

62497795
>>62497528
Sslinkie1 is at it again

62497824

two paths for a crypto
>be full privacy and cyberpunk
>be full authority approved

62498098

62498300

realsalad: Chainlink advised SEC on crypto, law makers on Clarity Act
realsalad: Chainlink partner with DTCC, Swift and other institutions
>>62498297

62498304

62498366

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 142 replies — 127.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 142 • Updated: 2026-07-19 12:58 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496329
Oracle is active.
There are no coincidences.
This is a banking product.
For Banks.

Who here can find me the Everest episode of Chainlink Tonight?

Just want to confirm the Everest webpage linked to a Dept of Defense info page.

62496405
>>62496329
Oracle contract was lost. Bob already said as much when he alluded to certain partnerships not working out

62496425
>>62496405
He never said they lost Oracle contract you fucking retard.
To think he wouldn't prioritize the biggest software company in the world is fucking retarded and you are a fucken retard.

62496426
>>62496425
Just stop coping and be happy he got Mica. Oracle is dead and all evidence proves that

62496769
>>62496426
another nail to the evergay casket

62496941
Just want to confirm the Everest webpage linked to a Dept of Defense info page.

This has been asked for before and it’s long gone just like Jace/Bob taco meet up footage Bob wanted deleted. Lots of hush hush kek, probably nothing burger as nothing good Everest happens here

62497284
>>62461988
Piece of shit coin can’t even barely pump off mica

62497302
It will bull flag here next 5 years then make next move in 2032

62497334
This is the saddest thread in /biz/ right now. Just one or few bagholder samefagging to bump the thread, preaching about promised features & partners that cannot be verified.

Here's some verifiable facts:
- Everest preach about identity but that is basically as far as the "edit your profile" text fields in their wallet. It also took days to have the platform verify any identity document because they do this manually, probably by a freelancing jeet.
- Their facial recognition is now handled by Sumsub because their inhouse recognition was so ass that you could get recognized as someone else or locked out your wallet after a haircut.
- the only RWA Everest have so far is the GEMS thing that more or less Viarpee collaterizing a stupidly illiquid asset (their own emerald product which btw cannot compete with synthetic equivalent)
- There's ZERO use of the ID token right now outside the fabled "B2B partner". They said ID used as gas, but if you do try to move ID from Everchain to Ethereum it actually used ETH as gas, and it doesn't use ID for gas during staking in Everchain.
- ID token was supposed to be used for VNO but 5+ years after ICO, this bare minimum of the utility is nowhere to be found.
- Their fiat on-ramp could work one day and breaks the next hour without notice. The rates also not very attractive once you compare it with on ramp provider available through metamask, etc. Also nobody use their on-ramp widget other than in Everest wallet itself.
- You can't even send assets in their wallet without editing URL.
- You won't see or find anyone other than Bob working on Everest. Maybe Brad, but he was last seen years ago.
- Their newsletter which released every 6 months, barely feature verifiable data or quantifiable achievement.

At the very least linkies and xerpies already amass significantly more hodler to keep their thread organic. Overest has always pretend to be as 'useful' as Link which is pathetic given their current state.

62497358
>>62496941
Thank you.

62497362
>>62497334
1 ID = 1 LINK

62497396
>>62497362
Typical ever cuck cope

62497635
Retard they have an enterprise wallet for clients. This post sounds like someone who left after 2024 and is not paying attention to any details

62497666
>>62497635
Good job changing id saar so good

62497753
Hopefully Bob puts me out of my misery one day. I hate managing a daycare full of autistics who think they know everything bc token holder wallet is dog shit. Bob said it last year they don’t have bandwidth for perfect consumer wallet and enterprise wallet so teams time and energy goes into enterprise wallet until they get bigger. So yes it all works, just not for your bitch asses

62497850
>>62497753
Bro just stop coping and put your cuck cage on

62497867
I’m sick of the cuck cage, I want out of this matrix and Everest will do it. All the old bitter holders that took 6 figure losses and clearly have not moved on since they are commenting in here can go fuck themselves

62497871
Idk how to reply directly to someone but to the person shitting on consumer wallet: you would not be here commenting on Everest 4chan post if you had any bit of success in crypto. Whatever you find next will also rugpull on you until you sell then take off just like ID well

62497958
>>62497867
You do understand that everyone sees your 4ch ID, right? Right???

62497995
Yes you stupid goy I am nWDGiV1T. I’m sorry but you guys are pathetic fudders, try harder

62498030
>>62497958
>47 posts by that ID
KEK how heavy you think that fucks bags are

62498225
>>62498030
The heaviest bag. From his writing style, I know this anon is this one 'animal' user from everest telegram who bought since ICO, held through ATH, bought the token every month despite the chart & project silence, and diligently stake everytime even after knowing that stake rewards never ever outpace the price decline, and never sell.

He is not a whale, but his bag definitely cost the most. It was funny to see his unwavering belief and occasional tantrum, but now I can only feel pity.

62498287
>>62461988
they will all get MICA in the end because they need to tax everything

62498311
Is that why Binance could not get it and a shit ton of other coins that preached compliance. Sure thing brother, everyone gonna get it in the end for taxed. Dumb ass MiCA 2.0 is even stricter, it’s not getting easier from here idiot hahahahhaa. Most of Defi will be wiped out or forced to use licensed payment solutions

62498556
Sipping on some woodford reserve because I know I will have the last laugh here. Nobody laughs at me and calls it a day, I will be right in the end. Can’t wait to revisit this thread in 2030, have a nice night all

62498568
>>62498556
You will be bald by then retard

62498575
I actually do not stress and try to meditate/energy harvest for ID token so my full set of hair will still be around once bull flag is complete end of 2030

62498583
I am the whale, and I am a very nice whale with diamond hands. Any other new whales are more than welcome here, 1.4m mc with MiCA CASP to operate in Europe. They will be attacking DTCC settlements with that Kick Start Award from Stellar as the in. 99.5% down from ATH, I’m literally doing you idiots a favor and NOBODY is doing an inch of research and just calling me a bag holding retard instead. Do you know how fucking frustrating that feels to me?? Search Everest ID on Twitter and look at Teams posts from last 5 years, that is good start. This project is over most people’s heads, it takes too much time to fully understand it so the retards chase memes instead. One day, the Yin Yang will balance out in crypto too

62499388
>>62498583
its time to sell

62499407
My brother in Christ, my target is many dollars, not 1 cent

item.title: at the peak of the dotcom buttle nasdaq's PE ratio was 80 today its 30 and retards are expecting a 50% crash at a time w… — 28 replies — 48.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
at the peak of the dotcom buttle nasdaq's PE ratio was 80 today its 30 and retards are expecting a 50% crash at a time w…

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-07-19 13:30 UTC

OP

at the peak of the dotcom buttle nasdaq's PE ratio was 80

today its 30

and retards are expecting a 50% crash at a time when incredible new technologies are being developed

Early replies (first 3)

62497460
How could there be 19 year old indigenous people? Aren't they like 450 years old?

62497461
>>62497460
serious question are you retarded and how are you using a computer

62497479
>>62497461
How is my question any more retarded than your thread?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62497515
If every bubble would have the same markers then it would be incredibly easy to spot them and they wouldn't happen

62497522
Incredible new technology is almost a necessary component of a bubble.

62497523
>>62497453
And these... incredible new technologies... are they in the room with us right now?

62497524
moral of the story, PE isn't the only metric

62497536
It will be obvious only in hindsight

62497540
>>62497524
its literally the only thing that matters

you can pull these meme correlations out of your ass all day but how much a company earns now and will earn in the future is what gives it its value

62497547
>>62497453
>incredible new technologies
it's a chat bot.... that hallucinates... and btw it requires $100M of GPUs and it costs you $20k/mo for """token spend""""

62497552
>>62497540
A chatbot company that also blows up rockets has a PE of -100 and is worth 2 trillion dollars

62497567
>>62497552
spacex is literally 1 company

im not saying there isnt a single overvalued company out there. im saying the MARKET is not a bubble.

>>62497547
and yet, its still better value than your average soiboy programmer who previously demanded a 500k salary or team of jeets

62497602
>>62497567
As you probably already know, most of the top tech companies are doing circular financing between themselves. This creates earnings (E) out of thin air and suppresses PE ratios. No guarantee they can keep doing this into the future.
Just one example:
Google's Q1 net income pumped 63%(!!) YoY. But half of their net income gain was due to "income from non-marketable equity securities". Basically, their stock holdings in OpenAI and Anthropic appreciated. Without this appreciation their net income would have not increased 63% YoY but instead been negative. And if/once OpenAI/Anthropic valuations drop, then Google will start to post losses instead.

They know people are looking at PE ratio now. Thus they adapt and start manipulating the ratio. This creates complacency in normie stock investors and allows for a bubble to happen. Next bubble they will have adapted in some different way again.
>But it's only tech stocks that are doing this circular financing, not the whole SP500
Mostly, yeah, but tech is now something like probably over half of SP500

62497605
>>62497567
>and yet, its still better value than your average soiboy programmer who previously demanded a 500k salary or team of jeets
i'm not a codetranny but my understanding is it increases productivity by maybe 20% in real life, specific to codetrannydom. so it's another case of jewish greed, where shekelberg's $300k/yr programmers (who generate the company profits of $2M/year per employee) can now do the work of employees who would otherwise earn $300k/yr * 1.2 = $360k/yr.

62497636
>>62497453
>new technologies are being developed

62497643
>>62497605
>i'm not a codetranny but my understanding is it increases productivity by maybe 20% in real life
maybe if you are a retard

62497937
>>62497643
>https://getdx.com/blog/ai-productivity-gains-more-modest-than-expected/
>SAAAR I AM DOING THE LAKH LOC CALLED AS 10X DEVELOPER

62497961
>>62497461
baited

62498007
>>62497937
look at figure 2 dumbass.

most retards dont even understand how to use ai. they will be filtered out into unemployment in the coming years

62498281
>>62497460
Some of those children buried under the churches were rescued, they're still alive actually and continue their education today. It's a Canadian miracle.

62498310
>>62497453
According to posters on twitter, Micron at its current 5 forward P/E is overvalued because something something earnings peak but price falls

62498312
>>62497515
NO YOU STUPID FUCKING FUCKER
IT'S VERY EASY
A BUBBLE IS WHEN SHIT IS OVERVALUED BY MULTIPLE FACTORS BUT DOESN'T HAVE THE PROFITS TO BACK IT UP

62498327
>>62497453
Incredible new technologies that are

COMPLETELY FUCKING COMMODITIZED

How the FUCK is OpenAI worth a trillion dollars when Fable-level AI has now been open-sourced?

Where the FUCK is it getting that much revenue?

62498329
>>62498007
ask claude to explain what a percentile is, lil zoomer.

62498355
>>62497540
30 p/e is still high

62499431
>>62497567
PE ratios are literally the same as they were during the dot com bubble and this dude is saying we're not in a bubble

62499455
>>62497453
stop being optimistic. read the room anon. you are on 4chan. majority of people here in their early 20s with no money and no future prospects. act accordingly. people here want to hear how shit every is and how everything is going to crash so they can get into the game at good time.

item.title: GMI? — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
GMI?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-19 14:01 UTC

OP

>26
>Investing steadily for one year and four months
>Teetering between ~17-20% return given the season
>No reason to believe I cannot keep contributing
Am I GMI?
I would be a bit happier because the original plan with my family was to receive ownership of the house and mortgage while keeping the low decades old 3% interest rate my parents had with the bank, but I think they might lose that soon
Other than that angle and some student loan debt that is only starting to gain interest now, this seems good for me

Early replies (first 3)

62493882
>>62493031
why did you take on student loan debt. I mean, if you are making mad money I guess it was worth it, but still.

62493926
>>62493882
Because I have no skills and there’s no way to get hired without a degree
Luckily had tons of scholarships so think I only owe $20,000

62494032
But should I be happy with this return
Seems good to me
Only thing higher that I’d feel still relatively safe in is TQQQ

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62494706
>>62493031
You are a rich man

62494815
>>62494706
I make 50k a year and live at home

62498125
how much do i need to get in here to start that fire bullshit

62499479
>>62498125
same

62499521
One year and 4 months ago you got in at a market correction so you timed it perfectly. We all made good returns the past 2 years unless you are pants on head retarded. Look at the market chart. Wouldn't take this as the average, but you are doing well above average for your age.

item.title: Need feedback on a website idea (beginner here) — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Need feedback on a website idea (beginner here)

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-19 13:56 UTC

OP

Disclosure: This is my own project idea. I'm only asking for feedback and criticism, not promoting.
Hi. I'm a CS graduate, but I know very little about modern web development and AI, so I'm looking for honest criticism rather than compliments.

I have an idea for a platform combining crowdfunding, collaboration, and community decision-making.

The idea:

- Anyone can post a real-world problem or a solution.
- Anyone can submit startup or creative ideas (movies, TV, anime, manga, comics, games, music, books, inventions, social projects, etc.) without first trying to satisfy publishers, investors, or large corporations.
- Instead of a few companies deciding what gets made, the public decides what they actually want.
- Investors, donors, and fans fund projects they believe in.
- Developers, designers, artists, writers, marketers, researchers, and others can join based on their skills.
- Progress is public, and the community can suggest improvements and help solve problems.
- AI matches people to projects, recommends collaborators, and generates development roadmaps.
- Long-term goal: help ideas become companies, products, scientific projects, charities, or creative works while giving ordinary people more influence over what gets created.

I'm not trying to replace Kickstarter or GitHub. I want a platform where good ideas can find funding, talent, and supporters.

Questions:
1. Does anything already exist that's very close?
2. What's the hardest technical challenge?
3. What features are essential?
4. What features should I avoid?
5. If you had to build an MVP alone, what would you build first?
6. Is this realistic for one person over several years?
7. Should I make it open source so others can contribute, or keep it closed until an MVP?

Assume I'm starting almost from scratch with React, Next.js, AI agents, databases, authentication, and cloud deployment.

Please be brutally honest. I'd rather hear why this won't work than waste years building the wrong thing.

Early replies (first 3)

62499503
>>62499237
>Please be brutally honest. I'd rather hear why this won't work than waste years building the wrong thing.
This is very nice, can be from AI slop up to perfect meritocratic algorithmic system. Objectively, it doesn't actually matter.

The real answer is depending on which tribe you belong to: /biz/raeli or goy/biz/?

62499508
>>62499503
>The real answer is depending on which tribe you belong to: /biz/raeli or goy/biz/?

If you are a bizraeli, then your fellow mountain turks will help you fund and build this and rake in heavy bags.

If you are not part of the chosenite tribe, they will steal this from you, or just destroy it from the outside.

The technical details are 0% important for your future. Not trying to demoralize, but you need a tribe before you continue.

62499511
>>62499237
>- AI matches people to projects, recommends collaborators, and generates development roadmaps.
This is already planned, it's a form of techno communism, AI will direct the whole central planning, including media production and consumption and arbitrate immediately any diverging edges.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: ecash — 7 replies — 6.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
ecash

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-19 12:59 UTC

OP

I'm only going to post this once, for two reasons

1. I feel a duty to inform you fags given I've been lurking this board for so long.
2. I want to be able to point back at this post forever, the first eCash alpha, for pure self pleasure.

Bitcoin is hardforking on the 22 aug. Not the 110 bullshit, but eCash, a continuation of the Bitcoin project without all the gatekeeping and baggage. You havent heard of it because its not loud, its the quiet next move for the money that matters.

For the newfags I'll break it down simply. Bitcoin has the ability to do softforks, a 100% safe, backwards compatibility, opt-in, ignorable upgrades that Satoshi left the ability for. The reasons that they arent used is a simple fact that Bitcoins leadership has been compromised, and its mindshare is being wasted on purposeful dead ends like Lightning and ARK. Simple opcodes like OP-VAULT, OP-CAT, and BIP-300 & 301 havent been merged and shit like Taproot has is insane. The focus is to redirect fees to corporations on top instead of the network itself.

eCash is an exact fork of Bitcoin Core, with only softfork being added. Being always compatible with Bitcoin Core, inheriting all the future work for free. BIP-300 & 301 softforks are initially added to allow for Drive Chains. Merge mined Sidechains for Bitcoin that enable Bitcoin to actually function and Hyper Bitcoinization to actually happen, while preserving the security model, and keeping the base chain easily verifiable.

Future generations will see Bitcoin as V1 of something that became V2: eCash on August 22nd. The realization will be slow and painful, but extremely profitable for those who know. Its necessary that the Bitcoin project continue, with or without the name

Early replies (first 3)

62499074
Your thread answered my question about which Bitcoin continuation projects should I dumped my hard earned money on next? I was in the dark but now I see the light. Thank you faggot.

62499078
>>62499074
Its the continuation because unlike every other scam you're getting 1:1 your Bitcoin holdings out of 21m, but it actually does things. It will beat Bitcoin, youre welcome, fag

62499082
>>62499072
I'm going to mine it like a total pig with my BitAxe 601.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62499097
>>62499078
This very good bitcoin saar

62499106
It's 2026 you stupid asshole, no one cares about crypto anymore.

62499396
>>62499072
I thought eCash (XEC) forked years ago? Amaury led the development. What the heck, is there another ecash now?

62499409
An ecash just flew over my house. I'll put some money on it though. Thanks for the hot tip.

item.title: As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy… — 69 replies — 39.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy…

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-07-19 12:20 UTC

OP

As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy from us. You get a Yuan swap line. You settle trade imbalances in gold or Yuan and use our settlement systems.

What can the US do? All they have is threats against the likes of Argentina. China has the vital goods they need at the right prices. The will continue to do this and kill dollar dominance.

Theres no other choice or enter an inflationary spiral.

Early replies (first 3)

62488083
how does this play out in 20 years? half the countries buy using Yuan? US goes back to manufacturing cuz dollar falls? Americans finally lose weight?

62488086
>>62488026
I thought Milei was anti-Chinese?

62488137
>Argentine peso devalues to zimbabwe-tier levels
>Hard for businesses to even buy USD because the market is so shallow and the currency so unwanted price discovery becomes tricky
>Milei's lolbertarian stance does little to appease markets, forex remains tightly regulated to avoid massive capital flights
>country with almost no industry or advanced services to export
>USD reserves run dry
>Great opportunity for China to dump it's surplus production, will probably be selling shit to Argentina at a loss in a government-set artificial exchange rate just as they do with Veneuela and African shitholes that struggle to access USD
>brown thirdie posts his paid shilling painting this as a massive victory for china and a sign of the dollar collapsing
We should have flags on /biz/. I'd call you a chink but I don't think chinks can even access this site.
>As I was saying yesterday.
Your thread died within 24hs on one of the slowest boards on this site. That should tell you something. Fuck off paid brown tankie.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496870
>>62495510
>It's raining on Buenos Aires

62496874
>>62495806
Now try sounding 20% less like a nigger, ChatGPT

62496966
>>62495806
Finally an intelligent person in this thread. I’m so tired of non-americans. Thank God for Elon Musk and Donald Trump.

62497156
>>62488026
>paying for imports in the currency of the exporting nation
Hey look it's fucking nothing.

62497161
>>62493633
>the solution is my bags
God I hate you bitcoin morons

62497162
Oh no another person saying the dollar is dying

I have only been hearing this daily for twenty years now

62497163
>>62495499

62497189
>>62488086
Anti-china stances in the region are more for propaganda consumption. In reality most try to find a balance playing the US and China off each other

62497196
>>62497161
their bag seems to be the solution to every problem

62497206
>>62496058
Hilariously, the country most probable to get nuked is the US... by nuking itself.

62497713
>>62488137
>a triple digit IQ post on /biz/
R A R E
A
R
E

62497725
I still don't understand how being a "global reserve currency" brings any benefit to me or the country

thanks

62497790
>>62497725
you'll find out sooner than later, you ignorant faggot

62497813
>>62488083
The bottom 90% of American are very poor and top 10% very rich.
Bottom 90% very brown and black and mixed.
Similar to what we have now but worse.

62497953
>>62488083
I hope so, even if we're 100% insular America could have one of the highest standards of living.

62498057
>>62493633
>The solution is the asset controlled by chinese data centers.
brilliant!

62498075
>>62488086
He is just a nihilist jew who wants to see Batman dead.

62498206
>>62488137
EXTREMELY good post.

62498282
>>62496252
They publicly SAID they are going for it. Meanwhile, they are stacking gold in unknown but very large amounts, streamlining gold import/export regs, opening exchanges, and curtailing paper gold trading. Does that all seem like random events to you? Doesn't to me.

62498284
>>62497162
How much value has the dollar lost in those twenty years?

62498292
>>62497725
It soaks up currency so the government/banks can create and spend massive quantities of new currency with minimal inflation.

62498381
>>62498282
The Chinese operate on an extremely long time scale. “Going for it” could be “In fifty years we aim to be the world reserve currency.” I have no doubt they’re going for it; they’re going for everything. The fundamental problem they face is that they are still an export economy and are not yet equipped to be the global financial hub (hell, there is barely an alternative to SWIFT and even then, its only used in a few trades between countries). They still manage their currency and artificially inflate/deflate the yuan as they see fit. I understand the argument that they want to be gold-backed, but that’s a long ways away. You’re jumping the gun.

62498391
>>62498292
yea so that non-whites can get welfare and boomers can get health care

62498444
>>62498391
The non-meme answer to your question is that much of America’s modern history is inseparable from its identity as the world reserve currency. The property, infrastructure, quality of life, etc. that you may feel are decaying are decaying *relative* to how good things were, and “how good things were” is the result of being the world reserve currency. So you may look around now and say you don’t see the benefits, but the reality is that all of contemporary American life is in the shadow of a litany of benefits, decades and decades of benefits.

62498492
>>62498381
To evaluate China's strategy, you must take into account how badly the USA is managing its financial system. Really all China needs to do is sit back and wait while the US burns down its own house. All roads lead to massive inflation of the USD. At some point the world is going to start feeling the pain and then China, massive exporter that it is, will only need to say "10% discount for paying in our new gold-backed yuan." Then 20%. Then 50%. Then "We no longer accept USD." It might take a few years, but the USA is incapable of stopping it. No shooting, no shouting, just economic power, and the fruit of decades of American greed and corruption.

It WILL happen. You say "long ways away". Maybe. I say the interest on the US national debt will consume ALL Federal revenue in less than ten years. And that will be the turning point (if we make it that far). The bridge is out and there is zero political will to stop the train. And when the USD crashes, China will be ready to pick up the pieces.

62498507
>>62498391
You are seeing the end days. From WWII until the early 80s, America had an economic boom like the world had never seen and it was a flood that raised all ships. But it was unsustainable. It gave us the k-shaped economy. And now it is breaking.

China saw that prosperity and wants it. Keep in mind that the CCP has made some colossal blunders with its central planning. And it will likely eventually bungle a gold-backed currency. But before that happens, gold stackers will do very nicely thank you.

62498511
>>62498444
This is correct.

62498595
>>62497725
Try being british or Swedish and you'll see what it's like to have a weak currency

62498877
>>62488026
>>62496108
>China wants that. They have said it publicly.
then why is the CNY/USD rate fixed? 1 USD = 7 CNY

>>62498282
>They publicly SAID they are going for it.
post source. also, post your bags of CNY.

this whole argument falls when you consider that single fact.

62499351
>>62498492
Your timeline is just too aggressive. Debt on interest will not surpass GDP in ten years.
>Then "We no longer accept USD." It might take a few years, but the USA is incapable of stopping it. No shooting, no shouting
This is where you’re definitely wrong. There will be shooting. If you think the US will politely surrender its financial position and quietly slip into economic collapse, you are mistaken.

OP

Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in now.

62483366

62483376

62491183
>>62483350
ok, everyone who is not OP
how low will it go?
will it ever rebound?
a good price to pick up shares?

62491228
>>62491183
Wait for what the orange jew says in 1 minute. If he's shilling some retarded war or increasing government spending in a manner to enrich his friends come back in six months. If he miraculously announces a ceasefire, come back in 6 days when he's threatening to glass them again. If the ceasefire somehow holds wait for the lockup period to end, timelines are available, and after majority is sold then observe and wait for floor to develop. If the economy is not functioning due to WW3 and an energy crisis followed by a recession buy a company that makes money instead. How do you fairly value a company that's prospectus is entirely memes, 18 pages of rocket photos and one net positive business drowned by 5 cash burning ones? You can't, no one can. Wait until its not dominating the news cycle and then buy it. I bought coinbases IPO, and had to wait quite some time to make money. Even if a company or sector is good, IPOs are best left to idiots. Buy things that are hated but for bad reasons, look at gold or uuuu as an example rather than financial advice. Everything bounces back eventually unless it doesn't. SPCX is hated for good reason and its barely even begun yet. Your goal shoul be to baghold as little as possible and we are entering a period where it may take 5+ years if you buy the wrong thing.

62491258
>>62491228
>Wait for what the orange jew says in 1 minute. If he's shilling some retarded war or increasing government spending in a manner to enrich his friends come back in six months. If he miraculously announces a ceasefire, come back in 6 days when he's threatening to glass them again. If the ceasefire somehow holds wait for the lockup period to end, timelines are available, and after majority is sold then observe and wait for floor to develop. If the economy is not functioning due to WW3 and an energy crisis followed by a recession buy a company that makes money instead. How do you fairly value a company that's prospectus is entirely memes, 18 pages of rocket photos and one net positive business drowned by 5 cash burning ones? You can't, no one can. Wait until its not dominating the news cycle and then buy it. I bought coinbases IPO, and had to wait quite some time to make money. Even if a company or sector is good, IPOs are best left to idiots. Buy things that are hated but for bad reasons, look at gold or uuuu as an example rather than financial advice. Everything bounces back eventually unless it doesn't. SPCX is hated for good reason and its barely even begun yet. Your goal shoul be to baghold as little as possible and we are entering a period where it may take 5+ years if you buy the wrong thing.
Are you ok? This post sounds like you need to take some sort of medication. Orange man does not in fact control everyone in the whole world. surely, you can make some analysis of this company, its fundamental, the human psychology around it, or any other of a number of variables without trying to screed about spraytan man. do you have a discord? because you post seems to heavily imply you do.

62491327
>>62491258
>analysis
I did, but its worth noting what oil prices did in 2008 and what followed. If you don't understand that price action can be affected by 2 or more factors simultaneouly then I suggest you ignore everything I said and consider $120 the new floor.

62491346
Desperate

62491495
>>62491183
120 is the floor but it will be $200 by EOY

62491881
>>62491495
Ok. Why

62491884
>>62483350
>Buy SPCX
SpaceKEKS

62491889
>>62483350
i rarely post here but ill save some newfags some money...


everyone should wait about 6 months, then decide.


way too early now. lots of down comin

62492490
>>62491495

62492529
>>62483350
It would do well if it didn't add xAI to the pile which is a money black hole just like every other AI

62492905
>>62483350
If it drops below 100 I'll consider it.

62492909
>>62491495
Floor bros....the floor keeps lowering

62493017
>how did you arrive at that price target

62493049
>>62492529
That's why it went public. To sell the AI top, and get CAPEX moneis. He needed to piggyback xAI on two hyper successful, wide moat businesses.

62493824
>>62490969
It's probably the other way around. Terrestrial networks will cover the bulk of the market, and satellite will likely remain a niche market for wherever terrestrial coverage is poor.

Laser interconnections in the vacuum of space are lower latency due to the higher speed of light in vacuum, although hollow core fiber mostly negates that advantage

62493846
>>62493049
What's the wide moat of Starlink? Competitors are already launching. The competitors will have fewer satellites, which means they will be able to serve fewer customers per square kilometer, but that doesn't really matter much, because the bulk of the space internet market is low population density regions.

62494909
>>62492909
>>62492905
>>62492490

62497303

They’ve been saying this stupid shit since 2001. Are you a fucken zoomer or something? If the rural areas do need it, how the fuck can they afford starlink which is $300/mo. You stupid fucken double nigger

item.title: Wagie, your daughter would have to suck my landchad nasty penis if you missed again the rent! — 11 replies — 23.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Wagie, your daughter would have to suck my landchad nasty penis if you missed again the rent!

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-19 13:37 UTC

OP

Wagie, your daughter would have to suck my landchad nasty penis if you missed again the rent! I would like it when your wife and daughter team up and fighting to eat the penis while I record it on the POV camera then live streaming to the internet.

Early replies (first 3)

62499026
idk i'm not in america but aren't there like tens of millions of pissed off people lurching into poverty who have nothing to lose? if you do what you wrote in your post the probabilities of you ending up strung up naked in some middle-of-nowhere woods begins to skyrocket. your mindset looks like it is from 1980s america and now it is halfway through 2026. but i'm not american so i can't be sure but just from what i read online it seems like heaps of people are unhappy and have nothing left to lose because they feel like they have nothing to live for. and you acting like you wrote in your post would just give so many of them a reason to see you later down the track in some random place nobody has even been to and for whatever reason it just ends there, like until some time later and then yeah but it is just not gonna be the same. idk though as i said i am not american and only going off what i have seen online recently with people being unhappy and having nothing to live for and all that. i think strange things happened in the 70s in america but i can't be sure. but yeah OP if you were smart and had a sense of self-care you should not be writing what you wrote or living like that at all.

62499029
>>62499012
>Most normal landlord

62499032
yuck didnt read all that

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62499115
>>62499012
In my village this how things go down. Timmy ANGRY his daughter spread the cheeks.

62499184
>>62499026:
Yo, welcome to the craziest, most cringe-overload clown world ever—Clownlandia 3000! Imagine a place where the sky is literally a neon rainbow vomit, and the streets are paved with glittery fart clouds and cotton candy explosions. Everywhere you look, giant goofy clown faces are plastered on skyscrapers, grinning like they just saw your worst dad joke. The ground? A wild mess of rainbow stripes, sparkles, and confetti cannons going off every five seconds. It’s like a never-ending circus nightmare on steroids.

Now, check this: I’m the main event—30 years old, looking fly as hell, full head of luscious hair that defies gravity, and I’m built like a Greek statue dipped in chocolate—tall, muscular, and just oozing confidence. I walk through this madness like I own the joint, and I got a wicked offer for all y’all clown freaks: "Rent free for a night, in exchange for a night." Yup, no cap, no joke, straight-up deal. The clowns and jugglers are staring at me like I just spoke alien gibberish, but I don’t care—because I’m that dude.

So, what’s the move? You gonna roll with this insane offer, or is this just another cringey meme in the biggest clown show the universe’s ever seen? No cap, no filter—just pure, unfiltered cringe chaos.

62499256
>>62499012
Reddit parody thread.

62499359
>>62499256
MADARCHOOOOOOD DO NOT REDEEM THE PENIS

62499361
>>62499012
hey the HVAC unit died again. i know, you replaced it just last month -- it's almost as if i'm purposefully sabotaging it. HAHAHA, just kidding. anyways, it needs fixed ASAP or i can legally withhold rent.

62499404

62475257

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62488091

62488156

62488664
>>62487536

62488668

not possible

62489527
>>62483295
New hype chain lol

62489716

ahem

62490257
>>62488664
Babylon's probably the best place to keep up with BTCFi right now.

62490309
>>62488156
not 39k? Lol

62491802
Lfg

62491816
Anything around the 200 week moving average is a bargain.

I'm DCAing heavily at these levels. I want to get rid of all my cash by October.

62492766
>>62490257
Hard not to when they're working with partners like a16z crypto, Utila, and Aegis. Each one brings something different to the ecosystem.

62493410
>>62491816

62494059
>>62492766
Something tells me we're only seeing part of the picture. Wouldn't be surprised if there's more to come.

62494064
>>62493410

62494358
>>62475235
I already have a decent bitcoin position and will be dca'ing more whenever it's below the 200 week. It's lame and boring but it works. My guess is that it bottoms between the 200 and 400 week MAs.

>>62475258
Dunk on ben all you want he's mostly right most of the time.

62494434
>>62494358
I'm not dunking, his videos are great.

62494839
>>62494358
Boring works. If you want to do more with your BTC, native staking already exists. TBVs look worth checking out once they're live.

62494846
>>62490309

62495219
>>62494434

62495227
>>62489716

62495280

62495641
>>62480931

62495924

item.title: I won my NEETbux court case and got $90K of back pay deposited into my bank account. — 22 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I won my NEETbux court case and got $90K of back pay deposited into my bank account.

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-07-19 11:23 UTC

OP

I won my NEETbux court case and got $90K of back pay deposited into my bank account. I now get 1700 per month, no taxes.

What should I do with my money? Someone told me I should movie to flyover country like Ohio and buy a fourplex with an FHA mortgage, so I can live in one unit and collect rent from the other three. Note that SSDI has no means testing for income or assets. And I can collect it outside the country.

Thoughts?
>hardmode: no crypto

Early replies (first 3)

62498475
>>62498458
fake story

62498477
you could literally get your dick sucked off a story like that

62498481
>>62498458
Not how that works. Fake retarded story.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62498484
>>62498475
>>62498481
How about you point out what you think is fake instead of just calling it fake?
I had to go through years of denial and appeals to get it. I had to hire a lawyer.

62498494
>>62498484
The he would have said not to disclose the settlement amount. There is no recorded settlement matching your fake story.

62498508
>>62498494
It isn’t a settlement; I won on appeal. Besides, it is an administrative process, not a tort action. All of it is not releasable by the government due to the Privacy Act. I have no restriction.
Please take law 101 before trying to sound intelligent.

62498510
>>62498508
Got any proof?

62498522
>>62498510
Of course I do. Award letter, SSA-Treasury deposits in my bank account, etc. but I’m not gonna give anyone shit. That isn’t the purpose of this thread.

62498533
>>62498522
Serious question, if you're well-versed in the law, and I assume you possess above average intelligence, why do you collect welfare?

62498537
>>62498533
>why does someone who is determined to be disabled collect disability insurance benefits?
It’s a mystery.

62498565
>>62498533
He basically came here for attention.

62498586
>>62498458
how did u do it? I think i have a good chance since ive been admitted over 5 times to psych wards.. i lost my last job due to mental illness literally went insane and said some shit to my boss which probably made him hate me

62498622
>>62498458
>>62498484
>>62498508
>>62498522
>>62498537
You can't get "back pay" unless you have a job that you worked and didn't get paid for. The only part of this story that is true is that you are a NEET.

62498782
>>62498586
Just read online about how to gather your records and apply. Have some money set aside for experts. Retain an attorney when they deny you.
>>62498622
Wrong. Here’s an answer from AI (I asked for it to be understandable for someone with a 75 IQ, like you):
Prompt: Is it possible to receive backpay when granted Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI)?

Answer: Yes. When Social Security approves SSDI, it may owe you money for earlier months when you qualified but had not yet been paid. This money is called backpay.

Usually:

* Social Security does not pay for the first five full months after your disability started.
* Payments can begin with the sixth full month.
* SSDI may also cover up to 12 months before you applied, depending on when your disability began.

Example: Social Security decides that your payable benefits should have started 20 months ago. It may give you those 20 missed monthly payments as one backpay payment, minus any lawyer’s fee or other required deductions.

62498810
>>62498782
No you're dumb.

62498858
>>62498782
yeah i'm sure they handed you half a decade worth of "backpay"

62498860
>>62498458
can you give a qrd on your disability and how you got it and if its someting you an teach other anons like me? ive been NEET for the last 6 years but have no income itd be nice to get something back from all the taxes i paid for the 2 decades of wagecucking i did

62498875
>>62498860
>can you teach me how to be disabled?
i don't think that's how it works bud
good news though if you're stupid enough to ask a question like that then you're at least mentally disabled

62498969
>>62498810
>I have no argument so I’ll just say “no” and call him a name, like a literal child
Kek.
>>62498858
Here’s an AI response for you:
It happens more often than people realize. If someone’s claim takes years because of appeals, Social Security can award benefits retroactively to the established onset date (subject to the statutory rules). The back pay is paid as a lump sum once the claim is finally approved. Five years is uncommon, but it’s absolutely possible in cases with long delays.
>>62498860
> ive been NEET for the last 6 years but have no income itd be nice to get something back from all the taxes i paid for the 2 decades of wagecucking i did
You’re running the risk of applying too far from the time you worked. Just read up on everything there is to know about SSDI (pay attention to Date Last Insured, in your case). Make sure you fill out the application perfectly. Have a few grand set aside for expert opinions. When SSA denies you, hire a good SSDI lawyer. And wait.

62499151
>>62498586
Lol, relax schitzo. Its a larp

62499255
why is it so easy to apply for retardbux. i guess you can walk you can talk you can use a computer wtf where is you in need for tardbux? you can do everything a normal person can do so how you get extra care in form of money? autism is just a diagnosis your doc gives you and then? why one gets benefits for being fit for wageslaving i dont get it

62499279
>>62499255
Boomers and socialists. Just stop paying taxes. Go full independent and never declare an income ever again; the boomer ponziconomy is on its way out, it has been factually bankrupt for 40 years anyway, the only thing keeping it afloat is cashflow from wagies getting automatically extorted on their paycheck.

item.title: ITT: We pretend we’re zoomers — 23 replies — 6.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: We pretend we’re zoomers

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-07-19 10:41 UTC

OP

>Why would I ever buy a house? I'd rather pay rent forever and invest the difference™. Sure, I don't actually invest the difference because I'm always "waiting for a better entry," but in theory I'd be richer. Delayed gratification is for boomers. I need maximum flexibility so I can move across town every 12 months after my rent goes up another 11%.

Early replies (first 3)

62494878
>>62494855
>If I make some money, maybe I'll convince a landwhale or a pajeeta to give me some pussy.

62494884
>>62494855
Unc is chopped

62494895
>>62494855
>pay rent forever and invest the difference
this is delayed gratification

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62494897
>>62494855
zoomers don't invest because they know only insiders get rich
until something like crypto can give everyone crazy returns for no reason they won't touch investing

62494901
>>62494895
Renting during your younger years and investing in the market is way better than owning some overvalued house. The market has always outperformed real estate for almost a century

62494965
>>62494855
>why yes, a cryptocoin named NOGDICK sounds like a proper investment. i'll use my mom's credit card to- anddd my stack is worthless now

62495099
>>62494878
Face>>>>>>>>>Height>>>>money
But you wont understand as a fat balding 40 year old, probably manlet too.

62495287
>Why are homes so expensive? I'm single, have never dated, and have zero marriage prospects. I need a 3 bed 2 bath home so I can entertain my hobbies of playing video games and masturbating.

62496393
>>62494855
>invest the difference

There was no difference if you bought in the 2010s.

62496400
>>62494901
The market does not out perform real estate when real estate is bought with leverage.

You can buy conventional with 3% down, 0% down with VA or USDA.

62496546
>>62496400
>when real estate is bought with leverage.
options dont provide leverage?

62496574
>>62495287
He's literally me.

62496576
>>62494855
>Is living in your vehicle really that bad

62497583
>>62496393
>why are boomers like this it isn't fair

62497590
>>62496393
duhhh my heckin' SF apt costs $4.2k/mo

62497592
>>62494855
Zoomers have retarded nigger clown hair, unc. Update your meme.

62497608
>Boomers RUINED the world with their progress and medical advances, now they live too long it's not fair waa waa I want papa's house so I can be a sexual deviant in 4k UHD on his broadband

62497611
>>62495287
Bedroom, goon cave and home gym. Perfect setup.
T. Late Millennial

62497620
>I'd rather pay rent forever and invest the difference
My mortage and upkeep is cheaper than what rent would be. Combine that with payments towards the principal and interest being tax deductible here and its a free €500+ every month.

62498128
>>62495099
NicheMaxx

62498407
>>62494897
>zoomers don't invest because they know only insiders get rich
And yet they constantly bitch about boomers, who have spent their whole lives investing in the most normie ways possible and are now well off. It's the admixture of brown DNA causing the stupidity imo.

62498414
>>62497620
People never mention the biggest thing here. When you rent, that money goes poof forever. When you own a house, you're really just deferring that money to yourself in the future, in the form of owning a hugely valuable asset. There's no comparison and I don't get why this rent vs. own argument even exists.

62499223
>>62494855
>College is a scam. I'm gonna make money from dogecoin and forex trading.

item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK? — 118 replies — 5.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?

Open thread • Replies: 118 • Updated: 2026-07-18 18:15 UTC

OP

Every time I see the UK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The FTSE is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the UK. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The UK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

Early replies (first 3)

62481973
>>62481958
the problem is that its being filled with immigrants who are living off tax payers money
thats the gist of why everyone is so pissed off here

62481986
>>62481973
So just right wing racebaiting?

No, I think it runs deeper than that. Most people have not personally had negative experiences with migrants, and reading the Daily Mail does not count.

I am trying to angle from a financial perspective too. People talk about the UK like it is a poor country but it is one of the richest countries on the planet. I think perhaps they are just spoiled and do not know what poor countries are really like. I blame social media.

62481994
>>62481958
>fantastic healthcare for minimal cost
my nan lived in birmingham and she came down with leukaemia and was gone within a couple of months. She never even got to see a doctor because the muslim immigrants were getting seen first. if i had something to lose i wouldnt even dare post this for fear of legal reprimand.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62490813
>>62490585
I'd ask where that is but I wouldn't want to blow it for you
Assuming its not a larp, godspeed anon

62490838
Sometimes I feel like responding to Europoors. Like I live in a capital city just minutes on foot to restaurants, shops, grocery stores. I pay less than 1k USD for my apartment. I can walk to work. I feel 100 percent safe, and could carry a firearm or knife if I so pleased but I am not a pussy. I often think about replying to Europoors and then I remember this meme.

62490850
>>62490585
Great post. A brit brangs about being able to walk/cycle outside and not getting stabbed.

62492082
>>62490850
bitter

62492388
>>62492082
Bitter about what?

62492412
>>62487400
>pictured: the entire UK fleet right before being crippled by their annual necessary repairs

62492424
>>62481973
>here
You need to get out before youre arrested for posting that

62492526
>>62492388
In context of the thread I was hardly bragging. I dare you to post where you live so we can have a go too

62492845
>>62492526
I live in a very expensive area created by corrupt political nepo insiders for corrupt political nepo insiders. Everybody has 2-3 newest Audi or mercedes SUVs and several millions in the bank. All the houses are 3,000+ square feet and have gardens. But not me, I'm an outsider here, and everybody knows that because they all know each other and that I'm not part of their club. All the neighbors are professional dogshit cunt parasites. So yeah you live in a better place.

62492892
>>62490838
>and could carry a firearm or knife

Love it how you're so compelled to mention this but are too afraid to use them for real because you'll be locked up.

Feel free to use it in a school or mall though. Seems like the average American pastime.

62492960
>>62492845
why would you move there? Sounds stupid

62493072
>>62481958
>Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs.
You can neither walk nor talk safely.
It's a beautiful country, it WAS a nice culture otherwise.
Now, if you have a daughter, or are a fuckable woman, you will get raped if not tortured by some gang, if you complain you will be arrested for racism, if you protest you will get beaten by the same worthless bobbies who refused to help, and it's all part of the meanest large scale society destuction, ethnic replacement, power grab and money grab back to feudalism ever in European history.
The economy is not better.

62493208
>>62492960
Because I'm a poorfag retard, it's not my place and I can live here for free.

62493518
>>62493072
what a load of tripe. 'Pakistani grooming gangs' weren't actually abducting and raping women you stupid retard. They were grooming them

62493619
>>62493518
even if that isn't a lie, "oh jeez, that's so much better, thanks!". you fucking clown, kys

62493666
>>62481958
if it's so great, stay there. stop running around and shitting everywhere. you flag is gay, and youor people are ugly af

62493751
>>62490585
>97% white
If you think it will stay that way in the next 20 years, you're delusional.
>>62482110

62493775
>>62481958
Because it run by Israel and occupied by Palestine

62493802
>>62481958
>Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?
out of the starmer frying pan into the burnham fire

62493836
>>62481958

62493843
>>62493751
do you think where you live will stay the same for 20 years too?

62493991
>>62493843
No, but that's completely beside the point because I wasn't the one bragging about living in some ethno state paradise.

62494575
>>62482009
This is an unacceptable post and the police have been informed.

62494624
Slimes abducted, raped, tortured and had dogs rape 250k British girls. If you think this is ok, you are a pedo globalist, a Jew, or a pedo globalist Jew.

62495552
>>62493802
>46 years of capitalist exploitation
>widely accepted that the country has declined since then
>somehow, this is not capitalisms fault
>in fact, it's socialisms fault
>somehow
>even though the nordic countries have done really well under socialism
>and america is a warzone full of drugs and homeless people
Yes, what we need is MORE corporate exploitation. That wealth is just about to trickle down! We have been waiting since *checks notes* Reagan but it's just about to happen!

62495765
>>62481958
Uk is hell on earth. 2nd worst country in the world right after india

62495935
>>62482008
>>62482025
>>62484965
Lmao. The second the numbers look bad, suddenly metrics don't matter.

GDP per capita. Median income. Salaries. Productivity. They're all "bad metrics" now.

The nice thing about GDP, salary, height, BMI, IQ tests, or any other quantitative measure is that the number still exists whether it makes you seethe or not.

Our poorest state is richer than your entire country on a per-person basis. Your doctors earn what our gas station managers make. Kneel.

62497286
>>62483303
Proportionality. Whites are still the majority. As it goes down, it'll become less sustainable, until it implodes.

62497297
>>62495552
>>even though the nordic countries have done really well under socialism
In what world are the nordic countries socialist? Are you retarded?

62497683
>>62481986
your country is being taken over by muslims. your women are raped en masse and you're worried about "right wingers". You are a weak man.
